concepts.title: External Environment Factors
concepts.description: The external environment includes factors such as political, economic, social, technological, legal, and environmental. These factors affect the business operations and decisions.

concepts.title: Characteristics of Business Environment
concepts.description: Business environment is dynamic, uncertain, and unpredictable. It is the sum total of all external and internal factors that affect the business.

concepts.title: PESTLE Analysis
concepts.description: PESTLE is a framework used to analyze the external environment. It stands for Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, and Environmental factors.

concepts.title: SOOT Analysis
concepts.description: SOOT is a framework used to analyze the internal environment. It stands for Strengths, Opportunities, Weaknesses, and Threats.

concepts.title: Confusing External and Internal Environment
concepts.description: Students often confuse the external and internal environment. The external environment includes external factors, while the internal environment includes internal factors such as strengths, weaknesses, and so on.

concepts.title: Types of Business Environment
concepts.description: There are two types of business environment: internal and external environment.

concepts.title: Importance of Business Environment
concepts.description: Business environment is important for business success as it affects the business operations and decisions.

concepts.title: Key Features of Business Environment
concepts.description: Business environment has some key features such as complexity, dynamism, and uncertainty.
